title: Investment ratios
summary: 9609 A Level — dividend yield, dividend cover, P/E ratio, and shareholder analysis.
learningObjectives: Calculate dividend yield and dividend cover.
Calculate and interpret the price/earnings (P/E) ratio.
Analyse returns from a shareholder perspective.
Evaluate investment attractiveness using case data.

simpleExplanation.title: Shareholder perspective
simpleExplanation.summary: Investment ratios help shareholders judge income return (dividends) and how the market values the company relative to its earnings.
steps: High P/E may reflect growth expectations.

flashcards.back: Total dividend paid ÷ Number of shares in issue.
Profit for the year attributable to ordinary shareholders ÷ Number of shares.
EPS ÷ DPS — how many times earnings cover the dividend.
High income return — but may signal low share price or unsustainable payout.
Little profit retained after dividends — risky if earnings fall.
Market expects future growth; expensive relative to current earnings.

sections.content: **Investment ratios** (10.2.5) complete **Topic 10.2**. Questions often give **share price**, **DPS**, and **profit/ shares** — calculate **EPS first**, then yield, cover, and P/E.

EPS = $\frac{\text{Profit for ordinary shareholders}}{\text{Number of shares}}$

Dividend yield = $\frac{\text{DPS}}{\text{Share price}} \times 100\%$

Dividend cover = $\frac{\text{EPS}}{\text{DPS}}$

P/E ratio = $\frac{\text{Share price}}{\text{EPS}}$

sections.question:
2 million shares in issue. Profit for the year $5 m; 40% paid as dividend. Share price $4.00.

Calculate EPS, DPS, dividend yield, dividend cover, and P/E.

sections.content: **Interpretation:** High yield may attract income investors but check **cover** — 2.5× means dividends are affordable. **Low P/E (1.6)** suggests the market prices the share cheaply vs earnings (or data is simplified). Compare with **competitors** and **prior year**.
Distinguish **shareholders** (yield, P/E) from **directors** (ROCE, liquidity) — match ratio to the stakeholder in the question.
